intro: Cleistogenes gatacrei, also known as Gatacre's grass, is a perennial grass species native to Australia and Southeast Asia. Traditionally, Gatacre's grass has been used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Cleistogenes gatacrei include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which have been shown to possess antioxidant and immunomodulatory effects. Gatacre's grass is commonly prepared in herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to support overall health and well-being, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
